---
title: "Evaluation"
subtitle: "Latency, cost, and accuracy benchmarks for the cascade pipeline"
date: today
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Evaluation Framework

The cascade architecture is evaluated across three dimensions:

1. **System Latency** — time-to-decision at each cascade layer
2. **Cost Economics** — theoretical cost comparison vs. pure MLLM
3. **Model Performance** — pseudo-label vs. ground-truth trained YOLOv8

---

## 1. System Latency

Latency is measured separately per cascade layer, because each represents a distinct cost / speed trade-off.

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-latency
%%| fig-cap: "Decision latency by cascade path"
flowchart LR
    INPUT(["Frame\ninput"])

    L1_OK["Layer 1 only\n~5 ms\n(95% of frames)"]
    L1L2_OK["Layer 1 + 2\n~20 ms\n(4% of frames)"]
    L1L2L3["Layer 1 + 2 + 3\n~3000 ms\n(1% of frames)"]

    INPUT --> L1_OK
    INPUT --> L1L2_OK
    INPUT --> L1L2L3

    style L1_OK fill:#dcfce7
    style L1L2_OK fill:#fef9c3
    style L1L2L3 fill:#fee2e2
```

### Expected Benchmark Results

| Path | Average Latency | % of Frames | Notes |
|------|------------|-------------|-------|
| Layer 1 only (no defect) | ~5 ms | ~70% | MSE < τ |
| Layer 1 → Layer 2 (detected) | ~20 ms | ~25% | conf ≥ 85% |
| Layer 1 → Layer 2 → Layer 3 | ~3,000 ms | ~5% | conf < 85% |
| **Weighted average** | **~170 ms** | 100% | |

::: {.callout-important}
## Cold-Start Penalty
Scaling Layer 2 from zero replicas takes **30–90 seconds** when the Container App has been idle. This is measured and reported separately as `cold_start_latency_s` and is **not** included in the inference latency figures above. In production, use `--min-replicas 1` during active factory shifts.
:::

---

## 2. Cost Economics

We compare two architectures for processing **100,000 frames**:

| Architecture | Description |
|---|---|
| **Pure MLLM** | Every frame sent to GPT-4o |
| **Cascade** | Layer 1 (AE) + Layer 2 (YOLOv8) + Layer 3 (GPT-4o for ~5%) |

```{python}
#| label: fig-cost-comparison
#| fig-cap: "Cost comparison: Pure MLLM vs. Cascade (100,000 frames)"
#| echo: true

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# ── Parameters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
N_FRAMES = 100_000

# GPT-4o pricing (Azure OpenAI, as of mid-2024)
GPT4O_INPUT_PER_1M  = 2.50   # USD per 1M input tokens
GPT4O_OUTPUT_PER_1M = 10.00  # USD per 1M output tokens
AVG_INPUT_TOKENS    = 2_500  # per request (few-shot + image)
AVG_OUTPUT_TOKENS   = 50     # per request

# T4 GPU pricing on ACA (approx, per vCPU-second)
T4_VCPU_SEC = 0.000024  # USD per vCPU-second
YOLO_LATENCY_S = 0.015  # seconds per frame on T4
YOLO_VCPUS = 8

# ── Cost calculations ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def gpt4o_cost(n):
    input_cost  = (n * AVG_INPUT_TOKENS  / 1_000_000) * GPT4O_INPUT_PER_1M
    output_cost = (n * AVG_OUTPUT_TOKENS / 1_000_000) * GPT4O_OUTPUT_PER_1M
    return input_cost + output_cost

def yolo_cost(n):
    return n * YOLO_LATENCY_S * YOLO_VCPUS * T4_VCPU_SEC

# Pure MLLM: every frame goes to GPT-4o
pure_mllm_cost = gpt4o_cost(N_FRAMES)

# Cascade: ~5% of frames reach GPT-4o; all flagged frames (~30%) go through YOLOv8
cascade_flagged_fraction = 0.30
cascade_escalation_fraction = 0.05
cascade_yolo_cost = yolo_cost(N_FRAMES * cascade_flagged_fraction)
cascade_gpt4o_cost = gpt4o_cost(N_FRAMES * cascade_escalation_fraction)
cascade_total = cascade_yolo_cost + cascade_gpt4o_cost

# ── Plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

# Bar chart comparison
architectures = ["Pure MLLM\n(GPT-4o only)", "Cascade\n(this project)"]
costs = [pure_mllm_cost, cascade_total]
colours = ["#ef4444", "#22c55e"]

axes[0].bar(architectures, costs, color=colours, edgecolor="white", linewidth=1.5)
axes[0].set_ylabel("Total Cost (USD)")
axes[0].set_title(f"Cost for {N_FRAMES:,} frames")
for i, (arch, cost) in enumerate(zip(architectures, costs)):
    axes[0].text(i, cost + 5, f"${cost:.0f}", ha="center", fontweight="bold")

# Cascade cost breakdown (stacked bar)
cascade_components = ["YOLOv8\n(T4 GPU)", "GPT-4o\n(5% escalation)"]
cascade_values = [cascade_yolo_cost, cascade_gpt4o_cost]
bar_colours = ["#3b82f6", "#a855f7"]

axes[1].bar(cascade_components, cascade_values, color=bar_colours, edgecolor="white", linewidth=1.5)
axes[1].set_ylabel("Cost (USD)")
axes[1].set_title("Cascade Cost Breakdown")
for i, (comp, val) in enumerate(zip(cascade_components, cascade_values)):
    axes[1].text(i, val + 0.5, f"${val:.2f}", ha="center", fontweight="bold")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("assets/cost_comparison.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

reduction = (1 - cascade_total / pure_mllm_cost) * 100
print(f"\nPure MLLM cost:  ${pure_mllm_cost:,.2f}")
print(f"Cascade cost:    ${cascade_total:,.2f}")
print(f"Cost reduction:  {reduction:.1f}%")
```

---

## 3. Model Performance

We train **two** YOLOv8n models and compare them on the Golden Test Set:

| Model | Training Data | Purpose |
|-------|--------------|---------|
| `yolo_pseudo_v1` | Pseudo-labels from GPT-4o (~1,420 images) | Production model |
| `yolo_gt_v1` | Human ground-truth labels (~1,420 images) | Baseline / upper bound |

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-model-comparison
%%| fig-cap: "Model training comparison methodology"
flowchart LR
    subgraph PSEUDO["Pseudo-Label Track"]
        PL["Pseudo-labels\n(GPT-4o annotated)"]
        M1["yolo_pseudo_v1\nYOLOv8n"]
    end

    subgraph GT["Ground-Truth Track"]
        GTL["Human labels\n(ground truth)"]
        M2["yolo_gt_v1\nYOLOv8n"]
    end

    TEST["🧪 Golden Test Set\n360 images\nground-truth labels"]
    EVAL["Evaluation\nPrecision / Recall / mAP50"]

    PL --> M1
    GTL --> M2
    M1 --> EVAL
    M2 --> EVAL
    TEST --> EVAL

    style PSEUDO fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#3b82f6
    style GT fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#22c55e
```

### Expected Results (to be filled post-training)

| Model | Precision | Recall | mAP50 | mAP50-95 |
|-------|----------|--------|-------|----------|
| `yolo_gt_v1` (human labels) | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| `yolo_pseudo_v1` (GPT-4o labels) | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| **Difference** | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |

::: {.callout-note}
## Hypothesis
We expect `yolo_pseudo_v1` to achieve within **5 percentage points** of `yolo_gt_v1` on mAP50, demonstrating that GPT-4o pseudo-labels are a viable substitute for expensive manual annotation at this scale.
:::

---

## 4. Full Cascade System Evaluation

The final cascade evaluation measures the **combined system** on the 360-image Golden Test Set:

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-system-eval
%%| fig-cap: "System-level evaluation flow"
flowchart TD
    TEST["360 Golden Test Images\n(with ground-truth labels)"]
    CASCADE["Run full cascade\nLayer 1 → 2 → 3"]
    METRICS["Compute Metrics"]
    P["Precision"]
    R["Recall"]
    F1["F1 Score"]
    LATENCY["Avg Latency\nper layer path"]
    COST["Actual API\nCost incurred"]

    TEST --> CASCADE --> METRICS
    METRICS --> P
    METRICS --> R
    METRICS --> F1
    METRICS --> LATENCY
    METRICS --> COST
```

**Target SLAs:**

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Recall (defect detection) | ≥ 98% |
| Precision | ≥ 85% |
| Avg latency (weighted) | < 200 ms |
| Cost per 100k frames | < $50 |
